intitule: Exercise 1: Identifying Active and Passive Voice (Ressource)

enonce.texte: Read the following sentences. Identify whether the verb is in the Active Voice (A) or the Passive Voice (P).

questions.texte: The janitor collects the garbage every Monday.
questions.reponse: A (Active Voice)

questions.texte: Many trees were planted in our school compound last year.
questions.reponse: P (Passive Voice)

questions.texte: The minister will open the new hospital next week.
questions.reponse: A (Active Voice)

questions.texte: This delicious dish is prepared by my mother.
questions.reponse: P (Passive Voice)

questions.texte: Someone stole my friend's phone at the market yesterday.
questions.reponse: A (Active Voice)

intitule: Exercise 2: Transforming Tenses into Passive Voice (Ressource)

enonce.texte: Transform the following active sentences into the Passive Voice. Pay attention to the tense (Present Simple or Past Simple).

questions.texte: The government builds many roads in Cameroon.
questions.reponse: Many roads are built by the government in Cameroon.

questions.texte: A famous architect designed the National Museum in Yaoundé.
questions.reponse: The National Museum in Yaoundé was designed by a famous architect.

questions.texte: Farmers grow plantains and eru in the South Region.
questions.reponse: Plantains and eru are grown by farmers in the South Region.

questions.texte: A violent storm destroyed several houses in our village.
questions.reponse: Several houses were destroyed by a violent storm in our village.

questions.texte: The headmaster does not allow mobile phones in class.
questions.reponse: Mobile phones are not allowed in class by the headmaster.

intitule: Exercise 3: Applying Passive Voice to Real-Life Contexts (Competence)

enonce.texte: You are a journalist for a student newspaper in Cameroon. You are writing a short article about the 'End of Year School Prize Ceremony'. Rewrite the following notes into complete sentences using the Passive Voice (Present Simple or Past Simple) to focus on the actions, not the people who did them.
images: A school prize ceremony in Cameroon. Students are receiving trophies and books on a stage. A headmaster is giving a speech at a podium.

questions.texte: The headmaster / to give / a speech of encouragement.
questions.reponse: A speech of encouragement is given by the headmaster.

questions.texte: Trophies and books / to award / to the best students in each subject.
questions.reponse: Trophies and books are awarded to the best students in each subject.

questions.texte: A special prize for civic conduct / to give / to the most helpful student.
questions.reponse: A special prize for civic conduct is given to the most helpful student.

questions.texte: The national anthem / to sing / by the school choir before the closing.
questions.reponse: The national anthem was sung by the school choir before the closing.

questions.texte: The ceremony / to organize / by the Parents' Association.
questions.reponse: The ceremony was organized by the Parents' Association.

enonce.texte: Each sentence below contains a mistake in the formation of the passive voice. Analyze the error and correct the sentence.

questions.texte: The children was taken to the zoo last Saturday.
questions.reponse: The children were taken to the zoo last Saturday.

questions.texte: English is teach by Mr. Atangana.
questions.reponse: English is taught by Mr. Atangana.

questions.texte: These beautiful masks are make by local artists.
questions.reponse: These beautiful masks are made by local artists.

questions.texte: The match was played in the stadium.
questions.reponse: This sentence is correct.

questions.texte: My phone were stolen yesterday.
questions.reponse: My phone was stolen yesterday.

enonce.texte: Evaluate the situation below. You are the secretary of your neighborhood development committee. Your neighborhood has a serious waste management problem. Formulate a 5-point action plan using the Passive Voice to explain what needs to be done. The first one has been done for you as an example.
images: A street in a Cameroonian town with piles of uncollected garbage and plastic bottles scattered around.

questions.texte: Example: (Collect waste twice a week) -> Waste should be collected twice a week.
questions.reponse: This is an example.

questions.texte: (Place more public bins in the area)
questions.reponse: More public bins should be placed in the area.

questions.texte: (Educate residents on recycling bottles and plastics)
questions.reponse: Residents should be educated on recycling bottles and plastics.

questions.texte: (Organize a monthly community clean-up)
questions.reponse: A monthly community clean-up should be organized.

questions.texte: (Fine people who litter illegally)
questions.reponse: People who litter illegally should be fined.

questions.texte: (Create a green space from a waste dump)
questions.reponse: A green space should be created from the waste dump.
